title: The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
summary: Ishaan Sehgal argues that agent identity and continuity should be centered on durable session logs rather than models, tools, or orchestration frameworks. He draws an analogy to save files in video games to illustrate that the log, not the compute or framework, is the persistent entity that defines an agent across different runtimes and machines.
writeup: Ishaan Sehgal of Omnara argues that an agent's identity and continuity reside in durable session logs rather than in models, tools, or orchestration frameworks. He uses save files in video games as an analogy: the log, not the compute or framework, is the persistent entity that carries an agent across different runtimes and machines. The framing makes append-only logs the unit of durability and portability, with context management built on the recorded session rather than on any single runtime.
